# UC-19.3.9 · oVirt / Red Hat Virtualization (RHV) Administrator Login and Privileged Action Audit Trail

> **Criticality:** High &middot; **Difficulty:** Advanced &middot; **Pillar:** Observability &middot; **Type:** Audit, Security, Operations &middot; **Wave:** Walk &middot; **Status:** Verified

*We keep a plain-language diary of who signed into the virtualization control desk and who pressed the buttons that move clusters, disks, or guests—so audits stay calm when someone asks what happened overnight.*

---

## Description

Correlates **oVirt-Engine / RHV-M** **`engine.log`** **`EVENT_ID`** audit tuples (**`USER_VDC_LOGIN(30)`**, **`USER_VDC_LOGIN_FAILED(114)`**, **`USER_VDC_LOGOUT(31)`**) with privileged-motion traces (**`USER_RUN_VM(34)`**, **`VM_MIGRATION_DONE(63)`** / **`VM_MIGRATION_FAILED(65)`**, **`USER_ACTIVATED_HOST(43)`**, **`USER_DEACTIVATED_HOST(516)`**, **`USER_ADD_CLUSTER(811)`**, **`USER_REMOVE_CLUSTER(813)`**, **`USER_ADD_PERMISSION(852)`**, **`USER_REMOVE_PERMISSION(854)`**, role events) across **AdministrationPortal**, **UserPortal**, and **`/ovirt-engine/api/`** REST surfaces, layering **AAA extension** families (**`ovirt-engine-extension-aaa-jdbc`**, **`ovirt-engine-extension-aaa-ldap`**, **`ovirt-engine-extension-aaa-misc`**) so security and platform teams retain a defensible timeline when **KVM / VDSM** estates change state.

## Value

Administrator repudiation evaporates once Splunk stitches **WildFly**-emitted **`EVENT_ID`** narratives to **PostgreSQL** **`audit_log`** columns—the same tuples regulators expect during access attestation—while still explaining noisy but honest maintenance (storage sweeps, migration windows, directory lockout recovery) without forcing analysts to mine the Java UI alone before an insider or broken automation rewrites **cluster**, **storage-domain**, or **VM** posture.

## Implementation

Ship **`engine.log`** with a **LINE_BREAKER** tuned to Java multiline stacks, tag **`ovirt:engine`**, mirror **httpd** **`access.log`** for portal versus API path context, optionally land **`ovirt:audit_log`** from **DB Connect** or **`api/events`** pollers using **`correlation_id`** keys, then schedule daily attestation dashboards and high-water alerts on **`USER_VDC_LOGIN_FAILED(114)`** density per **`admin@internal`** successors.

## Control test

### Positive scenario

A compromised **`cluster-admin`** principal fires **`USER_VDC_LOGIN_FAILED(114)`** bursts followed by successful **`USER_VDC_LOGIN(30)`** from a new workstation subnet, then **`USER_ADD_PERMISSION(852)`** granting **`SuperUser`** to a dormant LDAP alias—Splunk **`combined_risk_score`** climbs because **`login_failed_lines`** and **`privileged_motion`** spike together.

### Negative scenario

Monthly **`USER_RUN_VM(34)`** waves triggered only by **`cron`**-scheduled **`ansible`** **playbooks** inside approved **`automation`** subnets keep **`login_failed_lines`** at zero and carry **`correlation_id`** tags tied to **`CHG`** tickets—risk stays flat.

## Detailed Implementation

### Prerequisites
- **oVirt 4.5.x** upstream or **RHV 4.4** deployments where `ovirt-engine.service` writes **`/var/log/ovirt-engine/engine.log`** via **WildFly** while Apache httpd terminates `443` with `mod_proxy_ajp` to **`8080`**. Confirm **PostgreSQL** retains **`audit_log`** rows mirrored through **DB Connect** ([Splunkbase 2686](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/2686)).
- Splunk Universal Forwarder plus Splunk Add-on for Linux ([Splunkbase 833](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/833)) must read Engine logs without weakening **`SELinux`**—run **`restorecon -RFv /var/log/ovirt-engine`** after permission drift.
- Map AAA extensions deliberately: `admin@internal` rides **`ovirt-engine-extension-aaa-jdbc`**, directories bind via **`ovirt-engine-extension-aaa-ldap`**, and **`ovirt-engine-extension-aaa-misc`** handles Kerberos or certificate quirks—whitelist `authz` realms before blaming principals.
- Separate portals functionally: `AdministrationPortal` (**`/ovirt-engine/webadmin/`**) exposes destructive infra verbs; `UserPortal` (**`/ovirt-engine/userportal/`**) stays VM-self-service; **`/ovirt-engine/api/`** plus `/ovirt-engine/sso/oauth/token` carry automation unique from federated Keystone stacks elsewhere.
- Tie **`USER_ADD_PERMISSION(852)`** spikes to **`SuperUser`**, `ClusterAdmin`, **`DataCenterAdmin`**, `HostAdmin`, `NetworkAdmin`, `StorageAdmin` CMDB roles—not opaque GUID blobs.

### Step 1 — Configure **`inputs.conf`** monitors and sourcetype hygiene
(1) Publish **`local/inputs.conf`** monitors mirroring `dataSources`—keep **`engine.log`** as **`ovirt:engine`**, **`access.log`** as **`ovirt:httpd:access`**, notifier output as `ovirt:notifier`, `server.log` as **`ovirt:server`**.
```
[monitor:///var/log/ovirt-engine/engine.log]
index = virtualization
sourcetype = ovirt:engine

[monitor:///var/log/ovirt-engine/server.log]
index = virtualization
sourcetype = ovirt:server

[monitor:///var/log/ovirt-engine/notifier/notifier.log]
index = virtualization
sourcetype = ovirt:notifier

[monitor:///var/log/httpd/ovirt-engine/access.log]
index = virtualization
sourcetype = ovirt:httpd:access
```
(2) Tune **`LINE_BREAKER`** / **`TRUNCATE`** so `EVENT_ID:` tuples remain glued to **`java.lang`** traces—truncating stacks fractures **`privileged_motion`** counts when `WildFly` emits verbose faults.
(3) Optionally ship **`linux_secure`** when `gssapi` pilots align with `aaa-misc` extension proofs.
(4) Decide whether **`ovirt-engine-sdk-python`** REST pollers execute from Heavy Forwarder credential vaults versus Search Heads—never stash `engine-setup` secrets inside personal notebooks.
(5) Budget `indexes.conf` retention—Hosted-Engine maintenance duplicates `EVENT_ID` tuples until **`correlation_id`** dedupe macros reconcile upstream/downstream brokers.

### Step 2 — Optional `audit_log` ingestion (**DB Connect** or HEC)
(1) Grant read-only `SELECT` limited to `audit_log_id`, **`log_time`**, **`log_type_name`**, **`severity`**, **`user_name`**, **`correlation_id`**, **`cluster_name`**, **`data_center_name`**, `vm_name`, **`host_name`**, **`message`**—reject **`SELECT *` during forensic `VACUUM`** cycles.
(2) Align **`rising`** inputs so JDBC timestamps stamp **`_time`** faithfully—latency dashboards explain mismatched **`USER_RUN_VM(34)`** narratives versus **`audit_log` replicas lagging eleven minutes behind `WildFly`** buffers.
(3) Keep **`GET /ovirt-engine/api/events?search=`** parity whenever JDBC blocked ([REST guide](https://www.ovirt.org/documentation/doc-REST_API_Guide/)).
(4) Preserve **`correlation_id` JSON spelling inside HEC batches—camelCase drift breaks `transaction`** joins ([AAA overview](https://www.ovirt.org/develop/release-management/features/infra/aaa.html)).
(5) **`engine-setup`** OAuth rotations differ from **`ovirt-aaa-jdbc-tool`** JDBC resets—route LDAP outages through IAM bridges distinct from **`internal`** vault edits.

### Step 3 — Splunk correlation (**`spl` reference)
Paste `spl`** into hourly governance searches plus fifteen-minute SOC sweeps; macro-wrap **`index` when `virtualization_lab`** clones duplicate staging telemetry.

Search rationale highlights:
– **`EVENT_ID`** **`rex` tolerates `Message:`** reorder across locales because **`evt_num`** integers (**`USER_VDC_LOGIN(30)`, `USER_VDC_LOGIN_FAILED(114)`) remain authoritative.
– `portal_lane` distinguishes `AdministrationPortal` bursts from `UserPortal` self-service churn unrelated to infra sabotage.
– `aaa_lane` separates `jdbc`**, **`ldap`**, **`misc`** chatter—critical when **`USER_VDC_LOGIN_FAILED(114)`** reflects directory outages rather than guessed **`admin@internal` passwords.
– `privileged_motion`** merges **`VM_MIGRATION_DONE(63)`**, **`USER_DEACTIVATED_HOST(516)`**, **`USER_REMOVE_CLUSTER(813)`** without twelve unrelated alerts.
– **`combined_risk_score`** triple-weights **`login_failed_lines` yet still catches obsessive `USER_ADD_PERMISSION(852)`** streaks.

### Step 4 — Validate (**Administration Portal UI**, **`psql`**, **`curl`**)
(1) Verify against the RHV Administration Portal UI—under **Administration → Event Log, confirm `EVENT_ID`** + **`correlation_id`** pairs mirror Splunk rows before arguing parsers broke.
(2) **`curl --negotiate`** / **`--user`** probes against **`https://<engine>/ovirt-engine/api/events` anchor REST timelines beside `engine.log` buffers ([REST guide](https://www.ovirt.org/documentation/doc-REST_API_Guide/)).
(3) `psql`** checks **`severity`** integers (**`0` normal, `10` error, `20`** alert) versus textual **`Severity:` strings—do not silently downgrade `USER_REMOVE_PERMISSION(854)` urgency.
(4) Compare `USER_DEACTIVATED_HOST(516)`** bursts against **`fence` calendars—exclude rehearsed `DR` subnets flagged `environment=DRT`** inside lookups.
(5) Correlate **`/sso/oauth/token`** **`HTTP 200` bursts with benign `USER_RUN_VM`** spikes whenever Ansible rotates **`refresh_token` cadences faster than humans log in.

Extended instrumentation guidance (between Steps 4 and 5)
Optional HEC batches sourced from scripted `GET /ovirt-engine/api/permissions` pulls enrich `USER_ADD_PERMISSION(852)` investigations when `engine.log`** truncates narratives yet REST JSON preserves **`role`** identifiers. Pair **`ovirt:httpd:access`** **`uri_path` telemetry showing `/ovirt-engine/webadmin`** bursts against **`ovirt:notifier` spikes—broken SMTP relays sometimes mimic privilege floods despite steady `EVENT_ID` velocity. Mirror `postgresql`** slow-query cautiously—never substitute DB noise wholesale for **`audit_log` truth.

### Step 5 — Troubleshooting

- No data for rotated `engine.log`** inode swaps until UF reopens descriptors—bounce inputs after **`logrotate` rehearsals.

- `EVENT_ID`** extraction misses localized **`Message:`** bundles—fallback **`rex`** on **`evt_num` integers until parsers stabilize.

- `principal_norm='unknown_principal'`** everywhere—tighten **`User` `rex` or ingest structured `user_name`** from **`ovirt:audit_log`.

- `portal_lane`** unspecified—join **`ovirt:httpd:access`** **`clientip` tuples ±30 seconds around `_time` before blaming `REST` automation alone.

- Duplicate JDBC plus REST duplicates privilege totals—dedupe `audit_log_id`** macros prior **`stats`.

- `USER_VDC_LOGIN_FAILED(114)` clusters during IPA lockouts mimic stuffing until `idm` clears `policy`** relaxations—threshold per **`authz`.

- `VM_MIGRATION_FAILED(65)` floods usually trace `storage-domain`** timeouts inside **`vdsm`** logs—not APT indicators.

- **`SELinux` `AVC`** denies **`splunkfwd`** **`read`**—run **`audit2why`** before reinstalling UF.

- **`DB Connect` backlog stretches `_time` skew—pause paging until latency dashboards recover SLA.

- `OAuth`** churn after Ansible **`client_secret`** rotations resembles brute-force bursts—suppress documented automation CIDRs nightly.

- **`USER_REMOVE_CLUSTER(813)` bursts during `terraform` teardown mimic sabotage until SCM hashes converge—annotate `pipelines`** inside lookups.

- **`notifier`** storms amplify **`INFO`** chatter—validate **`ovirt-engine-notifier.service`** health before rewriting SPL thresholds.

Governance overlays
Quarterly reviews compare **`combined_risk_score` deltas against `QuotaConsumer`** dashboards—quota exhaustion legitimately amplifies **`USER_RUN_VM(34)` without implying lateral movement. `ovirt-engine-websocket-proxy.service`** TLS offload occasionally absorbs brute-force **`401` retries before `engine.log`** logs **`USER_VDC_LOGIN_FAILED(114)`, warranting `access_log`** companions whenever SOC insists perimeter telemetry disagree with JVM narratives.

Retention posture
Coordinate **`engine-config`** **`AuditLog` pruning intervals with Splunk `indexes.conf` frozen buckets—seven-year `audit_log`** governance collapses when PostgreSQL trims **`log_time`** sooner than Splunk expects.

Operator bridging
Maintain **`rhv_principal_owner`** lookups tying **`principal_norm`** strings to real pager rotations—Hosted-Engine VIP DNS relabel events rename hosts yet **`authz`** realms stay stable, preventing mistaken termination tickets.

## SPL

```spl
index=virtualization (sourcetype="ovirt:engine" OR sourcetype="ovirt:audit_log") earliest=-24h
| eval blob=coalesce(_raw, message, "")
| rex field=blob max_match=24 "(?i)EVENT_ID:\s*(?<evt>[A-Z0-9_]+)\((?<evt_num>\d+)\)"
| rex field=blob "(?i)Severity:\s*(?<engine_severity>INFO|WARNING|ERROR|ALERT)"
| rex field=blob "(?i)User\s+(?<principal>[\w\.@-]+)@(?<authz>[\w\.@-]+)(?:\s+cannot\s+login|\s+connected|\.)"
| rex field=blob "(?i)Correlation\s+ID:?\s*(?<correlation_id>[A-Fa-f0-9\-]{8,})"
| rex field=blob "(?i)(?<corr_bracket>\[[0-9a-fA-F\-]{8,}\])"
| eval correlation_id=coalesce(correlation_id, corr_bracket)
| eval principal_norm=lower(trim(coalesce(principal, user_name, 'unknown_principal')))
| eval portal_lane=case(match(blob,"(?i)/ovirt-engine/webadmin"), "AdministrationPortal", match(blob,"(?i)/ovirt-engine/userportal"), "UserPortal", match(blob,"(?i)/ovirt-engine/api"), "REST_API", match(blob,"(?i)/sso/oauth/token"), "OAuth_token_exchange", true(), "unspecified_engine_surface")
| eval aaa_lane=case(match(blob,"(?i)aaa-jdbc|aaa_jdbc|internal\\s+realm"), "aaa_jdbc_profile", match(blob,"(?i)aaa-ldap|aaa_ldap|active\\s+directory|freeipa"), "aaa_ldap_profile", match(blob,"(?i)aaa-misc|gssapi|certificate"), "aaa_misc_profile", true(), "aaa_unclassified")
| eval login_signal=case(evt="USER_VDC_LOGIN" AND evt_num="30", "login_success", evt="USER_VDC_LOGIN_FAILED" AND evt_num="114", "login_failure", evt="USER_VDC_LOGOUT" AND evt_num="31", "logout", true(), "other_login_family")
| eval privileged_motion=case(evt="USER_RUN_VM" AND evt_num="34", "vm_power_on", evt="USER_STOP_VM" AND evt_num="33", "vm_power_off", evt="VM_MIGRATION_DONE" AND evt_num="63", "vm_migration_complete", evt="VM_MIGRATION_FAILED" AND evt_num="65", "vm_migration_failed", evt="USER_ACTIVATED_HOST" AND evt_num="43", "host_activate", evt="USER_DEACTIVATED_HOST" AND evt_num="516", "host_deactivate", evt="USER_ADD_CLUSTER" AND evt_num="811", "cluster_create", evt="USER_REMOVE_CLUSTER" AND evt_num="813", "cluster_delete", evt="USER_ADD_PERMISSION" AND evt_num="852", "grant_acl", evt="USER_REMOVE_PERMISSION" AND evt_num="854", "revoke_acl", evt="USER_ADD_ROLE" AND evt_num="890", "role_grant", evt="USER_REMOVE_ROLE" AND evt_num="893", "role_remove", true(), "non_privileged_or_other")
| eval severity_bucket=case(evt_num="114" OR engine_severity="WARNING", "warn", engine_severity="ERROR" OR engine_severity="ALERT", "bad", evt_num="65", "bad", true(), "info")
| where login_signal!="other_login_family" OR privileged_motion!="non_privileged_or_other"
| eval cluster_name=coalesce(cluster_name,cluster), vm_name=coalesce(vm_name,vm), host_name=coalesce(host_name,host), data_center_name=coalesce(data_center_name,data_center)
| stats values(evt) as event_symbols, values(evt_num) as event_numbers, dc(authz) as auth_realms, values(portal_lane) as portals_hit, values(aaa_lane) as aaa_extensions_seen, latest(engine_severity) as latest_engine_severity, sum(eval(if(login_signal="login_failure",1,0))) as login_failed_lines, sum(eval(if(privileged_motion!="non_privileged_or_other",1,0))) as privileged_lines, latest(correlation_id) as sample_correlation, values(cluster_name) as clusters, values(vm_name) as guests, values(host_name) as hosts_seen, values(data_center_name) as datacenters by principal_norm
| eval combined_risk_score=(login_failed_lines*3)+privileged_lines
| sort - combined_risk_score
| head 200
```

## CIM SPL

```spl
| tstats summaries=t count FROM datamodel=Authentication.Authentication WHERE nodename="Authentication" AND (Authentication.action=failure OR Authentication.action=success) BY Authentication.user Authentication.app earliest=-24h
| where count>=5
| eval note="Populate CIM Authentication fields from parsed engine tuples before relying on this shortcut alone."
```

## Visualization

(1) **Matrix**: **`aaa_lane`** × **`login_signal`** heatmapped by **`login_failed_lines`**. (2) **Timeline**: **`privileged_motion`** stacked bars colored by **`severity_bucket`**. (3) **Sankey-style chord table**: **`principal_norm`** → **`clusters`** → **`guests`** for insider-threat hunts. (4) **Single-value**: rolling twenty-four-hour ratio of **`login_failed_lines`** to successful **`login_signal`** rows (`login_success`/`logout`) split by **`authz`**. (5) **Drilldown table**: **`combined_risk_score`**, **`event_symbols`**, **`portals_hit`**, **`datacenters`**, **`hosts_seen`** feeding ticketing workflows.

## Known False Positives

**Rolling datastore evacuation migrations** — engineers intentionally **`USER_RUN_VM`** / **`VM_MIGRATION_DONE(63)`** hundreds of guests while hosts drain; Splunk totals look like hostile sprawl unless **`change_record_id`** lookups mark the window. **`engine-setup` post-patch replays** — package updates rerun configuration wizards that emit fresh **`USER_ADD_CLUSTER(811)`**-like noise (or benign **Engine** restarts) though no new physical **DataCenter** appears; correlate with **`rpm`** **change tickets** before escalating. **Ansible Tower or AWX loops refreshing OAuth secrets** against **`/ovirt-engine/sso/oauth/token`** stack **`USER_VDC_LOGIN_FAILED(114)`** lines when **`refresh_token`** lifetimes skew—treat as automation, not brute force, once inventory shows the controller host. **FreeIPA / Active Directory lockout storms** — **`ovirt-engine-extension-aaa-ldap`** continues logging failures while **`kinit`** recovery works on the vault side; IAM noise should clear after **`password_expire`** fixes but still looks like attacker spray in raw counts. **Nightly snapshot pruning** paired with **`USER_STOP_VM`** patterns can resemble abusive power-off attempts when jobs touch the same **`vm_name`** repeatedly; validate **backup** product schedules. **Hosted-Engine live migration of the Engine VM** duplicates familiar **`EVENT_ID`** strings on both source and destination brokers—deduplicate via **`correlation_id`** plus **VDSM** host context to avoid twin alerts. **Certificate-heavy aaa-misc pilots** temporarily raise **`login_failure`** counts while browsers learn new trust anchors—track **PKI** **maintenance** feeds before opening **SOC** incidents. **DR site failovers rehearsed quarterly** replay **`USER_ACTIVATED_HOST(43)`** / **`USER_DEACTIVATED_HOST(516)`** stacks identical to sabotage drills—tag rehearsal subnets inside **`environment=DRT`** lookups.
